channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Transgender people are about 1% of the US population. Yet they're a political lightning rod
item.description: On the campaign trail, Donald Trump used contentiousness around transgender people's access to sports and bathrooms to fire up conservative voters and sway undecideds.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deputies won't face charges in deadly 2022 shootout on California highway, prosecutors say
item.description: Prosecutors in California say San Bernardino County deputies involved in a shootout that killed a teenage girl and her fugitive father will not face any criminal charges.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: White House may change how journalists can ask Trump questions
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Comedian Paul Rodriguez arrested on drug charge in California, police say
item.description: Rodriguez, 70, was booked at the Burbank Police Jail for narcotics possession and later released with a citation to appear in court on April 25, Sgt. Turner elaborated.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KRON4

item.title: Wisconsin appeals court won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters after attorney general sues
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin appellate court denied the state Democratic attorney general's request to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters at a rally planned for Sunday, just two days before a closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UPS launching online tool to show tariff costs to customers
item.description: “Until now, international purchases often arrived with an unpleasant surprise – an additional bill for unpaid import costs.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, ABC 4

item.title: Steph Curry scores a 3-book deal, starting with a 'personal reflection' on his life and career
item.description: The NBA superstar has agreed to release three books through One World, beginning Sept. 9 with “Shot Ready," which Curry is calling a “personal reflection” on his life on and off the court. The book also includes more than 100 photographs.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What to do before you quit your job
item.description: Thinking about changing jobs? You’re in good company. Job tenure is now the shortest it has been in over 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Rosie Cima, NewsNation

item.title: Protests against Elon Musk's purge of US government swarm Tesla showrooms
item.description: Crowds have protested billionaire Elon Musk’s purge of the U.S. government under President Donald Trump outside Tesla dealerships throughout the U.S. and in some cities in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Passenger flight and Air Force jet diverted from potential collision at DC airport
item.description: A U.S. passenger flight preparing to leave the nation's capital and an incoming military jet received instructions to divert and prevent a possible collision, officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's election order creates much confusion before the next federal election in 2026
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to change how U.S. elections are run is creating uncertainty for state and local election officials and worries about voter confusion before the next federal election, the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New wave of smaller, cheaper nuclear reactors sends US states racing to attract the industry
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — With the promise of newer, cheaper nuclear power on the horizon, U.S. states are vying to position themselves to build and supply the industry's next generation as policymakers consider expanding subsidies and paving over regulatory obstacles.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kidnapped 6-year-old girl discovered during traffic stop: troopers
item.description: A trooper's discovery during a routine traffic stop last month led to the rescue of a 6-year-old girl who had been kidnapped for weeks, according to the Kansas Highway Patrol.
item.dc:creator: Barry Owens, KTLA 5

item.title: Delta plane, Air Force jet nearly crash in 'loss of separation' during Arlington National Cemetery flyover
item.description: A Delta plane carrying 131 passengers nearly collided with an Air Force jet in a 'loss of separation' Friday leading up to a flyover at Arlington National Cemetery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon city at heart of high court homelessness ruling blocked from enforcing camping ban
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — An Oregon judge issued a preliminary injunction Friday blocking the city at the heart of a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on homeless encampments from enforcing its camping rules unless it meets certain conditions, as part of a lawsuit filed by advocates.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina wildfire keeps growing as firefighters protect homes
item.description: A large wildfire in the South Carolina mountains has doubled in size on each of the last three days. But fire crews have been able to keep the blaze away from structures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Voice of America wins in court, for now, as judge blocks Trump administration from firing staff
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Voice of America can’t be silenced just yet. A federal judge on Friday halted the Trump administration’s efforts to dismantle the eight-decade-old U.S. government-funded international news service, calling the move a “classic case of arbitrary and capricious decision making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Texas city almost entirely underwater amid rain storms
item.description: Nearly all of the city of Rio Hondo is underwater after the massive rain storms that hit the Rio Grande Valley on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Diana Eva Maldonado, NewsNation

item.title: A look at who has been detained or deported in a US crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters
item.description: Supporters of Palestinian causes with ties to American universities have been detained in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A second South Carolina death row inmate chooses execution by firing squad
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina death row inmate on Friday chose execution by firing squad, just five weeks after the state carried out its first death by bullets. Mikal Mahdi, who pleaded guilty to murder for killing a police officer in 2004, is scheduled to be executed April 11.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate Republican chair challenges Trump over emergency funding move
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine), the head of the powerful Senate Appropriations Committee, and her Democratic counterpart are challenging the Trump administration’s handling of emergency funding included in legislation passed earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Former El Paso Morgan Stanley financial adviser sentenced for multi-million fraud scheme
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) — A former Morgan Stanley financial adviser in El Paso was sentenced in federal court to 12 years in prison for committing a multi-million dollar fraud scheme, according to the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Western District of Texas. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, NewsNation

item.title: Musk to travel to Wisconsin ahead of judicial race, hand out $1M checks
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk announced late Thursday he would be traveling to Wisconsin ahead of a high-stakes state Supreme Court race to talk with voters and hand out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: NFL legend Randy White fights fentanyl after personal tragedy
item.description: Jesse Dempsey, a professional snowboarder, unknowingly took a pill laced with fentanyl, believing it was a prescribed painkiller for a knee injury.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dorman, NewsNation

item.title: Stanford, Cal and UCLA investigated in Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: The Trump administration has opened investigations into the admissions policies at Stanford University and three campuses within the University of California system, including UC Berkeley, UCLA and UC Irvine, the Department of Justice said Thursday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: First rain and then fire chase people from their homes in North and South Carolina
item.description: When Nicole Taylor and her family moved to their new home in the South Carolina mountains six months ago, the gorgeous view of Table Rock Mountain was the clincher.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers in major restructuring
item.description: In a major overhaul, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers and shut down entire agencies, including ones that oversee billions of dollars in funds for addiction services and community health centers across the country.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KRON4

item.title: Judge: High heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional” but did not order the state to begin installing air conditioners.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: How a 1970s Chicano art group defied the mainstream and made history
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When filmmaker Travis Gutiérrez Senger reflects on ASCO’s legacy, he quickly notes they were more than an art group; they created a movement, one with remarkable influence on Chicano art history. “That movement continues today, and it’s very expansive,” he says.
item.dc:creator: LESLIE AMBRIZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden’s ‘ghost guns’ crackdown
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the Biden administration’s regulation of “ghost guns” in a 7-2 decision Wednesday, rejecting a challenge brought by gun rights groups and several manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4
